rulings.Case Title: NAIFEH AZAR VS. PABLO E. RODRIGUEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants To Provide Response To Special Interrogatories Re: Set One
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2023 line 1. PLAINTIFF NAIFEH AZAR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants To Provide Response To Special Interrogatories Re: Set One is transferred to department 301 to be heard at 9:00 a.m. on January 17, 2023. Parties to provide courtesy copies of their respective filings to department 301 in accordance with the requirements of the department. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SF OAKLAND BAY, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. PORTSIDE MASTER OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, A NONPROFIT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Regarding Four Subpoenas Issued By Plaintiff

rulings.Case Title: SANG AU SU, AN INDIVIDUAL ET AL VS. NAM MUI AU, AN INDIVIDUAL ALSO KNOWN AS NAM MUOI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT NAM AU, PHAT CAM DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Hearing Required. No later than 9:00 a.m. on January 11, 2023 Jaynry Mak to file and serve a declaration stating that notice of contesting the December 9, 2022 tentative ruling was provided to Plaintiff's counsel between 3:00 p.m. and 4:00 p.m. on December 8, 2022. Any evidence of such notice, e.g. copy of an e-mail; phone print-out, to be attached to the declaration. If notice has not been provided as described above, Jaynry Mak shall file a declaration so stating. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCANS FOR SPORTS AND RECREATION VS. ARE-SAN FRANCISCO NO. 53, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT ARE-SAN FRANCISCO NO. 53, LLC, ALEXANDRIA REAL ESTATE EQUITIES, L.P. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint is DENIED. Based on the gravamen of the proposed new allegations, Cross-Complaint seeks to supplement the complaint (alleging facts occurring after the filing of the original cross-complaint). However, the request to file supplemental complaint is not made in the Notice of the motion and is not supported by legal authority. Cross-Complainant may file a motion for leave to file a supplemental complaint if necessary and proper given the procedural posture of this case. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 1633-1649 CABRILLO LLC ET AL VS. DAVID GRANT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT DAVID GRANT DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Defendant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that "the rent ordinance requires Plaintiff to alert the tenant that a written request [for additional payment] is required. SFRO 37.9A(e)(1)(C) does not support this assertion. Defendant further fails to show that the inclusion of the word "receiving" in the Notice addressed to "David Grant and any other occupants claiming the right to possess the premises" renders the Notice defective. =(501/RCE) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
